response.justification: Candidate is a full-stack/web software engineer with no demonstrated experience in SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator performance analysis, GPU/SIMD/MIMD architecture, or hardware-software co-design. The resume shows strong web, backend, and DLang work but none of the core competencies required for a Principal Performance Architect role focused on AI accelerator hardware. Brief C/C++ exposure at National Instruments (~7 months on a CVI IDE) is the closest adjacent experience and is far from sufficient.
strengths: Long track record (~14 years) shipping production software across multiple stacks
Some low-latency systems experience with DLang at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic
Brief C/C++ background at National Instruments on engineering tooling
concerns: No SoC modeling, IP modeling, or hardware-software co-design experience
No deep-learning workload, DNN training/inference, or AI accelerator experience
No GPU, SIMD, or MIMD architecture background; profile is web/full-stack, not hardware architecture

key_factors.factor: Missing AI accelerator / DNN performance background
No SoC / IP modeling or hardware-software co-design
Years of general software engineering experience
